title: N2 Weapons
wikitext:
[]: N2 Weapons             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Wed Oct 31, 2007 2:13 am Hello again, after 2 days of not going on this site and watching the series all over again, I noticed a peculiar "thing". Episode 1: The Nuclear Attack on Sachiel: http://evageeks.org/FGP/images/5/50/FGC_01_C076d.jpg http://evageeks.org/FGP/images/f/f5/FGC_01_C077d.jpg Episode 19: Eva 00's suicidal attack on Zeruel: Hmm..don't seem to have a picture of that. Anyway, what I'm trying to say is that the blast radius of the Episode 01 Mine was WAYYY bigger than the one in Episode 19. See, in Episode 19, the blast radius of the bomb was smaller than in comparison with the one in Episode 01. Misato's car in 01 got flung all over upside-down while the bomb was planted, say, over 50 km away. BUT, in Episode 19, the Bomb was planted in the Geo-Front, and hit the ceiling of it, but what I don't get is that why Kaji and Shinji weren't vaporized in the explosion. That suicidal attack was a mere 1-2 kilometer away. Shinji and Kaji weren't killed. But in Episode 01, Misato's car got flung upside-down and obviously would've been killed if any close. Opinions?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Oct 31, 2007 2:43 am good point. Maybe Angel explosions are just for show. the N2 explosion in EoE was pretty wicked, and would've probably destroyed Kaji's melons (if they weren't trampled already by the Eva Series and Asuka). And it wasn't nuclear. It was an N2 bomb. The most destructive non-nuclear conventional weap. in Japan's arsenal in the Eva. Univ. In ep19....I don't know if AT Fields absorb energy, but they do reflect incoming particles....hmmm....One possible explanation is that the shockwave was deflected [i] upwards[,i], hence the bouncing shockwave, and the wall of the GeoFront (The Black Egg) absorbed a lot of the energy from the N2 Blast? It took quite a lot of damage in EoE before it cracked. Also it is imperative to remember that there are several classes of N2 Bomb, and the possiblility that the one used on Sachiel might've not been the same that Rei used to kamikaze.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Wed Oct 31, 2007 2:46 am Oh yeah, (sorry about the nuclear thing), I remembered that there was different versions of the N2 weapon, jeez I forget so many darn things already! I should've thought about A.T Fields and all that kind of shit. Maybe that 2 day holiday wrecked my memory of Evangelion. :( . Anyway, I'm going to go to the FGP to find more.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 31, 2007 7:30 am Aaron says "Cartoon!".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 31, 2007 7:48 am TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Also it is imperative to remember that there are several classes of N2 Bomb, and the possiblility that the one used on Sachiel might've not been the same that Rei used to kamikaze.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Oct 31, 2007 4:20 pm Reichu wrote: N2 Bomb on Zeruel, N2 Mine on Sachiel.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Oct 31, 2007 4:31 pm The answer is simple: We actually hear Rei saying that she is extending Eva 00's A.T. Field to maximum; the idea is that Rei intentionally muffled the blast, because she didn't want to end up killing everyone inside the GeoFront. So it's *supposed* to not *appear* to be as powerful.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Oct 31, 2007 5:51 pm Is N2 Bomb > N2 Mine?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Oct 31, 2007 5:55 pm yup. figure, if Bombs can ttake out Hiroshima how much mines do?

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Wed Oct 31, 2007 6:14 pm Tasteless joke. Not funny really... So, Mines>Bombs in the world of Evangelion, apparently...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Oct 31, 2007 6:14 pm it wasnt a joke, it was serious

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Oct 31, 2007 7:44 pm then it wasn't really a good idea to mention the Hiro. Lots of people died there. Not a good thing. @ Shiro: ALright good to know

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 31, 2007 8:05 pm Mine, bomb...anyway, I'm content with the explanation that the bomb used on Zeruel was a weaker version of the ones used on Sachi and Israfel(Who were standing on the mines before the big kaboom), because of the simple reason that Gendo has one of those. Otherwise, it's always left to the UN to use an N2 on anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 31, 2007 8:42 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Mine, bomb...anyway, I'm content with the explanation that the bomb used on Zeruel was a weaker version of the ones used on Sachi and Israfel(Who were standing on the mines before the big kaboom)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 31, 2007 8:45 pm My mistake, depth charges. But it isn't the technical term for what bomb is what- you can have N2 bombs or mines or depth charges of varying power, just the same way you have 500lb or 1000lb or 4000lb bombs. These things are all high explosive bombs of varying size and power. Mix the huge size of the N2 Rei was carrying, and I get the impression that the bomb she tried on Zeruel was an old, weaker weapon Gendo obtained thanks to the fact that all present N2 weapons- bombs, mines, depth charges, or whatever other variations- are the precinct of the UN.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 31, 2007 9:01 pm Calling them all "bombs" gets confusing, though. I argued with V about this at length on the discussion page for what ended up being "N2 weapons" at the "Wiki", so I'm too lazy to reiterate here.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Wed Oct 31, 2007 9:44 pm I don't see why they should have varied explosive N2's. But it really depends on the target's A.T Field strength, Israfel's was obviously really weak, and I wonder why the UN didn't just plant a N2 mine in the direction of Matarael. That would've summed up the Angel pretty nicely I think. Although that would completely make the episode worthless. Anyway, can A.T Fields, like, say, concentrate all of the energy of bombs etc onto their directed target? Just wondering, because Rei was also using her Field to corrode Zeruel's AT Field. The bomb would've at least done some damage if the script allowed her to concentrate energy onto Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 31, 2007 10:06 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Aaron says "Cartoon!".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 31, 2007 10:19 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: The bomb would've at least done some damage if the script allowed her to concentrate energy onto Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 31, 2007 10:20 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: I don't see why they should have varied explosive N2's. But it really depends on the target's A.T Field strength, Israfel's was obviously really weak, and I wonder why the UN didn't just plant a N2 mine in the direction of Matarael. That would've summed up the Angel pretty nicely I think. Although that would completely make the episode worthless.

 have N2 bombs or mines or depth charges of varying power, just the same way you have 500lb or 1000lb or 4000lb bombs. These things are all high explosive bombs of varying size and power. Mix the huge size of the N2 Rei was carrying, and I get the impression that the bomb she tried on Zeruel was an old, weaker weapon Gendo obtained thanks to the fact that all present N2 weapons- bombs, mines, depth charges, or whatever other variations- are the precinct of the UN.
